aboutTool.title: What is AI Image Splitter?

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Answers to the most searched queries about online image splitters, Instagram grid makers, and printable A4 dividers.

whatIsImageSplitter.question: What is AI Image Splitter and why is it the best online image splitter?
whatIsImageSplitter.answer: AI Image Splitter is a free browser-based image splitter and photo grid maker. Upload any JPG, PNG, GIF, or WebP up to 50MB and it instantly divides the file into equal slices for Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest, Facebook, or printable A4 layouts. Because everything runs locally in your browser, it works fast, preserves quality, and never uploads your content.

howToSplitImage.question: How do I split an image online step by step?
howToSplitImage.answer: Follow this quick image splitter tutorial: 1) upload your image, 2) choose Default, 4:5, 3:4, or 1:1 plus rows and columns, 3) drag the locked crop box to the area you want, 4) click Split Image to generate previews, 5) download single slices or a ZIP package.

instagramGridMaker.question: Can I use this image splitter for Instagram grids and carousel posts?
instagramGridMaker.answer: Yes. The tool doubles as an Instagram grid maker and Instagram carousel splitter. Create 3x3 feed puzzles, 1x3 strips for reels, or portrait panels for stories, then download ready-to-post images that keep captions and swipe experiences aligned. Need pro-grade Instagram homepage grids that support the 2025 update? Visit our sister site igridmaker.com for that dedicated workflow.

splitImage3x3.question: How do I create 3x3, 2x3, or puzzle grids for Instagram?
splitImage3x3.answer: Switch to Grid mode, set rows and columns to 3x3, 2x3, 3x1, or any combo up to 10x10, then drag the crop box so the focal point stays inside every tile. If you leave the ratio on Default, you can simply click "Split Image" after upload to slice the full canvas. The preview confirms each tile before you publish.

supportedFormats.question: Which image formats and downloads are supported?
supportedFormats.answer: You can upload JPG, PNG, GIF, or WebP files up to 50MB and 8192×8192 pixels. Export the split images as JPG, PNG, WebP, or BMP and grab either individual files or a ZIP download for faster publishing.

freeToUse.question: Is AI Image Splitter free, unlimited, and watermark-free?
freeToUse.answer: Absolutely. This online image splitter is 100% free, has no hidden paywalls, requires no sign-up, adds no watermarks, and lets you split unlimited images for personal or commercial projects.

imageQuality.question: Will the split image quality stay sharp after exporting?
imageQuality.answer: Yes. The crop box locks your chosen area and the export pipeline keeps the original resolution, so even large carousels, posters, or Pinterest boards look as crisp as the source file.

privacySecurity.question: Is my content private when I split images online?
privacySecurity.answer: Your files never leave your browser. All processing happens locally, so the tool never uploads, stores, or inspects any image. Close the tab and everything disappears instantly.

mobileFriendly.question: Does this tool work on phones and tablets?
mobileFriendly.answer: AI Image Splitter is fully responsive, so you can upload from iOS, Android, or tablets, adjust the crop box with gestures, and download slices straight to your camera roll or cloud storage.

batchProcessing.question: Can I split an image into A4 sheets or multi-page prints?
batchProcessing.answer: Yes. Choose an A4-friendly ratio (Default or 1:1), set rows/columns to match how many sheets you need, and split the file. Each slice can then be printed on A4 paper, tiled across posters, or combined inside PDFs to match the popular "divide image into A4 sheets" workflows.

customDimensions.question: Can I lock 4:5, 3:4, or 1:1 ratios or set custom grids?
customDimensions.answer: Use the Aspect Ratio buttons (Default, 4:5, 3:4, 1:1) to lock the crop box, then dial in 1×1, 3×3, 2×3, 3×1, or any custom rows/columns up to 10×10. The split function honors both the ratio and the grid so every export meets Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest, or A4 print specs.

proTips.title: How to Get the Best Experience with AI Image Splitter
proTips.subtitle: Pro tips for split image online workflows

items.title: Match ratios to your platform
items.description: Before you split images online, pick Default, 4:5, 3:4, 1:1, or a custom grid that fits Instagram grids/carousels, TikTok stories, Pinterest boards, or A4 print layouts.

items.title: Drag the crop box to protect key subjects
items.description: After upload, reposition the locked crop box so your product, skyline, or text stays inside every slice; for banners or long stories, keep margins even across rows and columns.

items.title: Preview, then export ZIP + single slices
items.description: Use the instant preview to fine-tune rows and columns, then export both single files and a ZIP bundle so you can publish or archive without re-splitting.

items.title: Choose the right output format upfront
items.description: JPG works for social feeds, PNG keeps transparency, WebP/BMP suit lossless assets. Picking the format now saves conversion time later.

items.title: Leverage browser privacy and multilingual UI
items.description: All processing stays inside your browser, and you can switch languages (English, Portuguese, Indonesian, etc.) to guide teammates through the same 'upload → ratio → split → download' flow.
